page: Microsoft Azure E20as_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Eav6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 10 physical AMD EPYC 9V74 cores with a base frequency of 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 160.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and bzip3 single-threaded decompression scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached operations also demonstrate average throughput. For specialized workloads, text generation using a small 135M parameter language model achieves top-tier performance, whereas prompt processing for a large 70B parameter model is weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

The high memory density relative to compute resources positions this instance as a memory-optimized option. The lack of local storage requires external disk attachment, which represents a structural tradeoff for storage-intensive tasks. Given the 160.0 GB memory capacity and strong single-core performance, the instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, caching layers, and text processing applications that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure E20as_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Eav6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It provides 20 dedicated vCPUs across 10 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz, paired with 160.0 GB of system memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but supports premium storage. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench and stress-ng single-core results in the top 25%, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. Notably, small-model text generation reaches top-tier speeds, though large-model prompt processing is weak. This high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes the instance cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads, database operations, and text processing, provided local storage is not a prerequisite.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20as_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 20 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 160.0 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20as_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 20 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 160.0 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance, but no local storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high memory density for database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 20 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9V74 processors at 3.7 GHz
160.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU, requiring external premium storage attachment
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and text processing
